MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Apr-22-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Simmie Collins
Evanthia M. Spanos of BERRY & BERRY (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE AND RE-OPEN EXPERT DISCOVERY

No objections made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to continue the trial date and re-open expert discovery for the limited purpose of replacing plaintiff's expert Dr. Herman Bruch is granted. The trial date is continued to August 17, 2015 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Expert discovery is re-opened solely to allow the following: plaintiff may designate an expert in place of Dr. Bruch and such expert may prepare a report and be presented for deposition; if needed, defendants may supplement their expert witness designation limited to responding to plaintiff's new designation. Discovery is re-opened to allow any party to obtain plaintiff's updated medical records. This re-opened discovery closes on the new trial date. All other expert and fact discovery remains closed. No opposition filed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Velan Valve Corp.S Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-23-2014 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Velan Valve Corp.S Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-19-2014 Continued To Jul-30-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-10-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Arturo E. Sandoval of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / moving party The Wm. Powell Company
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Simmie Collins

RULING:
DEFENDANT THE WM. POWELL COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The Wm. Powell Co.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's negligence and strict liability causes of action are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also did not sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Plaintiff's objections to defendant submitting its own responses to standard interrogatories (Exhibit J) are sustained. Assuming, however, defendant did sustain its initial burden, plaintiff's declaration and Mr. McClure's deposition testimony create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (gaskets original to Powell valves) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration and Mr. McClure's deposition testimony are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Holly Sayers of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13678 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
